title: GitLab Agile Portfolio Management- Hands-On Lab: Collaboration via MRs and Wikis
headings: GitLab Agile Portfolio Management- Hands-On Lab: Collaboration via MRs and Wikis
Objectives
Task A. Set merge request approval rules
Task B. Create a merge request
Task C. Perform code review and merge changes
text:
GitLab Agile Portfolio Management- Hands-On Lab: Collaboration via MRs and Wikis

Estimated time to complete: 45 minutes

Estimated time to complete: 45 minutes

A merge request is a proposal to incorporate changes from a source branch to a target branch. Merge requests help you manage the changes that are applied to your code. In this lab, you will learn how to set up and manage merge request approval rules in your projects. You can learn more about merge requests in the documentation .

Approval rules define how many approvals a merge request must receive before it can be merged, and which users should do the approving. They can be used in conjunction with Code owners to ensure that changes are reviewed both by the group maintaining the feature, and any groups responsible for specific areas of oversight. See the documentation to learn more.

Task A. Set merge request approval rules

Navigate to your Family Budget Calculator project inside the Software > Core subgroup.

Navigate to your Family Budget Calculator project inside the Software > Core subgroup.

In the left pane, click Settings > Merge Requests .

In the left pane, click Settings > Merge Requests .

Scroll down to the Merge request approvals section and click Add approval rule .

Scroll down to the Merge request approvals section and click Add approval rule .

In the Rule name field, enter Infra team .

In the Rule name field, enter Infra team .

Task B. Create a merge request

In your Family Budget Calculator project, click Issues in the left pane.

In your Family Budget Calculator project, click Issues in the left pane.

Click into your Third-party financial services integration issue.

Click into your Third-party financial services integration issue.

Click on the Create merge request button at the bottom of the description of the issue.

Click on the Create merge request button at the bottom of the description of the issue.

In the Branch name field, change the text to read update-db-docs-perf-tools .

ick into your Third-party financial services integration issue.

In the Branch name field, change the text to read update-db-docs-perf-tools .

In the Branch name field, change the text to read update-db-docs-perf-tools .

Ensure that the Source (branch or tag) is set to main .

Ensure that the Source (branch or tag) is set to main .

Click Create merge request .

Click Create merge request .

Type Draft: Add performance tools to Family Budget Calculator documentation in the Title field. Putting ‘Draft:’ at the beginning of your merge request means that the merge request will not occur until it has been marked as ready. This is used to note that a merge request is not ready to be merged yet, and to prevent accidental merges. Note that the Mark as draft checkbox below the title will also check automatically when ‘Draft:’ is added to the title.

Type Draft: Add performance tools to Family Budget Calculator documentation in the Title field.

From the merge request details page, select Code > Open in Web IDE to edit files on the update-db-docs-perf-tools branch. The Web IDE is an advanced editor with built-in ability to commit to your repository branches. You can use the Web IDE to make changes to multiple files directly from the GitLab UI. See the documentation to learn more.

Task D. Create a wiki for project documentation

If you don’t want to keep your documentation in your repository, but you want to keep it in the same project as your code, you can use the wiki GitLab provides in each GitLab project. Every wiki is a separate Git repository, so you can create wiki pages in the web interface, or locally using Git. GitLab wikis support Markdown, Rdoc, AsciiDoc, and Org for content. Wiki pages written in Markdown support all Markdown features, and also provide some wiki-specific behavior for links. You can read more about wikis here .
